idea-plugin.name: GoDogLand BDD Runner
idea-plugin.description:
GoDogLand BDD Runner adds a focused Godog/Cucumber workflow to GoLand. It lets you work with Go BDD scenarios directly from .feature files, while keeping the test execution in Go's native test runner.

The plugin adds gutter actions for running and debugging whole features or individual scenarios, saves edited files before execution, supports custom environment variables, and maps Go test results back to the matching Gherkin scenarios.

Compatible with GoLand 2024.1+ / build 241+.

It also improves day-to-day navigation between Gherkin steps and Godog step definitions: Cmd+B from a feature step jumps to ctx.Step(...), Find Usages from a step definition lists matching feature steps, and inspections highlight undefined, unused, invalid, or ambiguous steps.

  • Run or debug Feature and Scenario lines from the gutter.
  • See pass/fail status icons beside recently executed BDD scenarios.
  • Navigate from .feature steps to Go step definitions and back.
  • Generate missing Go step definitions from undefined Gherkin steps.
  • Use GoLand's test tool window with links back to the related feature lines.
  • Configure environment variables used by Godog test runs.
idea-plugin.change-notes: Initial release with Godog/Cucumber gutter run and debug actions, feature and scenario status icons, step navigation, BDD inspections, quick fixes for undefined steps, GoLand test tool window integration, and configurable environment variables.

localInspection.displayName: Unused or invalid Godog step definition
